title: Anatomy of a Simple Scatter Plot
description: A visual breakdown of the key components used to construct a scatter plot for Exploratory Data Analysis, illustrating the relationship between two continuous variables.
relationships: The X-axis variable determines the horizontal position of a data point
The Y-axis variable determines the vertical position of a data point
Each data point represents a single observation with coordinates (X, Y)
The overall pattern of points reveals the relationship (e.g., positive correlation, negative correlation, no correlation) between the two variables
